message: Phase dependencies detected — using wave-based scheduling

FAILED: Code work is complete and correct: sandbox.policy.ts implements the documented API, allowlists match baselines, parity tests cover the required scenarios, ast-grep rule is registered, and all 17 baseline greywall test names pass. The phase fails on AC9 + AC11 because npm run check exits non-zero — the agents (agnix) sub-check reports 'agnix binary not found', a tool-availability failure the builder documents as environmental but cannot resolve from inside the active sandbox. Per the constraint that every phase-check.json show zero failures across all eight tools, the phase cannot exit until agents is restored to ok=true.

- .ridgeline/builds/fascicle-migration/phase-2-check.json: npm run check fails: agents sub-check returns exit 1 with 'agnix binary not found'. The handoff explains this as environmental — the worktree was prepared by `npm install --ignore-scripts` inside the active sandbox, which left node_modules/agnix/bin/agnix-binary missing, and both fallback recovery paths (GitHub releases via curl, cargo install agnix-cli) are blocked by the active sandbox's network policy. The constraint is non-negotiable: phase-<N>-check.json must show zero failures across all eight tools.

- .ridgeline/builds/fascicle-migration/handoff-02-sandbox-policy.md: No captured evidence that 'ridgeline build runs end-to-end with default sandbox enforcement' was verified at this phase exit commit. The legacy greywallProvider remains co-located in sandbox.policy.ts (so the legacy pipeline at claude.exec.ts still resolves its imports), but the handoff doesn't document an actual end-to-end run.
- .ridgeline/builds/fascicle-migration/phase-2-check.json: phase-2-check.json exists but shows ok:false because of the agents failure. The phase-exit gate requires zero failures.

Retrying (1/2) — Code work is complete and correct: sandbox.policy.ts implements the documented API, allowlists match baselines, parity tests cover the required scenarios, ast-grep rule is registered, and all 17 baseline greywall test names pass. The phase fails on AC9 + AC11 because npm run check exits non-zero — the agents (agnix) sub-check reports 'agnix binary not found', a tool-availability failure the builder documents as environmental but cannot resolve from inside the active sandbox. Per the constraint that every phase-check.json show zero failures across all eight tools, the phase cannot exit until agents is restored to ok=true.

FAILED: Phase delivers all 13 flow files, dispose-in-finally for 6 migrated entry points, ast-grep enforcement, plugin-surface audit, and a green npm run check (8/8 sub-checks ok, 1299 unit tests). Fails on AC4 (--help byte-equality drift, 8/22 commands drifted), AC5 (no .d.ts snapshot test), AC6 (no commander option-set snapshot test), and AC10's literal reference to src/cli.ts (file renamed to src/main.ts to work around a fascicle upstream bug). Substantial scope creep: project-wide ESM conversion was bundled into this phase.

- .ridgeline/builds/fascicle-migration/baseline/help/: 8 of 22 --help snapshots drift from .ridgeline/builds/fascicle-migration/baseline/help/. Specifically: ridgeline.txt, auto.txt, build.txt, plan.txt (and four others) differ. The drift is primarily caused by pre-Phase-8 feature commits (ace80dc 'feat(cli): add --require-phase-approval', 03a8ecb 'feat(settings): unlimited limits') that added new flags/text. AC4 requires byte-equality at this phase's exit regardless of cause.

- src/__tests__/: AC5 requires an automated snapshot test that re-runs `tsc --emitDeclarationOnly` over src/commands/*.ts and asserts byte-equality against .ridgeline/builds/fascicle-migration/baseline/dts/. Builder explicitly deferred this and relied on by-inspection verification. No such test exists in the test tree.

- src/__tests__/: AC6 requires 'A snapshot test of commander's parsed option set per command matches the Phase 1 baseline.' No such test exists. The implicit AC4 drift (new --require-phase-approval flag added pre-Phase-8) means the option set has also drifted.

- src/main.ts: src/cli.ts no longer exists. The file was renamed to src/main.ts to work around a fascicle 0.3.8 bin self-detection bug (fascicle/dist/index.js:7195 fires run_viewer_cli for any binary named cli.js). The SIGINT handler still exists in main.ts, so the spirit of AC10 is preserved, but the file name referenced in the AC and in constraints.md (Directory Layout: 'src/cli.ts — entry point') no longer matches the repository state. This is a fallback for an upstream tool failure documented in the builder's deviations and 'Notes for next phase' sections.

Retrying (1/2) — Phase delivers all 13 flow files, dispose-in-finally for 6 migrated entry points, ast-grep enforcement, plugin-surface audit, and a green npm run check (8/8 sub-checks ok, 1299 unit tests). Fails on AC4 (--help byte-equality drift, 8/22 commands drifted), AC5 (no .d.ts snapshot test), AC6 (no commander option-set snapshot test), and AC10's literal reference to src/cli.ts (file renamed to src/main.ts to work around a fascicle upstream bug). Substantial scope creep: project-wide ESM conversion was bundled into this phase.

FAILED: Phase migrates build/auto onto fascicle flows, captures dogfood evidence, and ships `npm run check` green. AC1–5, AC7–10, AC12–13 pass cleanly. Two ACs fail: AC6's SIGINT regression test spawns a minimal fascicle fixture instead of a `ridgeline build` child process — sub-criteria (b)/(c)/(d) are not actually verified — and AC11's parenthetical ("no command path imports it") is violated: src/commands/build.ts still imports `runPhase`, `buildPhaseGraph`, `validateGraph`, `getReadyPhases`, `hasParallelism`, and four worktree helpers from src/engine/pipeline/. Both deviations are explicitly acknowledged in the handoff but block the phase as written.

- src/engine/flows/__tests__/build.flow.sigint.test.ts: The SIGINT regression test does not start a `ridgeline build` in a child process as AC6 specifies; it spawns a Node child running a minimal fascicle fixture (sigint-runner.mjs) that contains no worktree creation, no claude subprocess spawning, and no log emission. Of the four sub-criteria, only (a) — exit code 130 — is meaningfully verified. Sub-criterion (c) is tested via `orphanCount` but in this sandbox `ps` is blocked and `orphanCount` swallows the EPERM and returns 0, making the `after <= before` assertion pass vacuously regardless of actual orphan behavior.

- src/commands/build.ts: src/commands/build.ts imports nine symbols from src/engine/pipeline/ across four files (lines 9, 11, 15, 16). AC11's parenthetical says the pipeline directory remains on disk but `no command path imports it` after this phase. The build flow delegates per-phase work to the legacy runPhase via an injection-style RunPhaseExecutor dependency, which is a continuation of Phase 8's pattern but is explicitly disallowed by AC11. The handoff acknowledges this as a deferral to Phase 11 (cleanup), but Phase 9's AC requires the work be done now.

Retrying (1/2) — Phase migrates build/auto onto fascicle flows, captures dogfood evidence, and ships `npm run check` green. AC1–5, AC7–10, AC12–13 pass cleanly. Two ACs fail: AC6's SIGINT regression test spawns a minimal fascicle fixture instead of a `ridgeline build` child process — sub-criteria (b)/(c)/(d) are not actually verified — and AC11's parenthetical ("no command path imports it") is violated: src/commands/build.ts still imports `runPhase`, `buildPhaseGraph`, `validateGraph`, `getReadyPhases`, `hasParallelism`, and four worktree helpers from src/engine/pipeline/. Both deviations are explicitly acknowledged in the handoff but block the phase as written.

FAILED: Phase 10 fails: Stryker mutation testing — the foundational tool this entire phase is built around — could not be executed under the active sandbox. Both required workarounds (RIDGELINE_SANDBOX=0 and vitest pool: 'forks') were attempted and verified non-functional; AC2 and AC3 are not numerically satisfied (both score files have captured: false, score: null). AC4's gate script accepts this DEFERRED state and exits 0, so no numeric assertion is ever made. AC1, AC5, AC6, AC7, AC8, AC9 pass. The spec is explicit that the absolute pre-migration score MUST be captured at this phase before Phase 11 deletes src/engine/pipeline/ — that capture has not happened.

- .ridgeline/builds/fascicle-migration/baseline/mutation-score.json: Pre-migration Stryker mutation score on src/engine/pipeline/ was not captured. Phase 10's Required Tools section mandates one of two paths (RIDGELINE_SANDBOX=0 OR vitest pool: 'forks' for coverageAnalysis: 'perTest'); the builder confirmed neither is sufficient under greywall — Stryker core's logging-server uses TCP-localhost IPC at @stryker-mutator/core/dist/src/logging/logging-client.js:20 that greywall denies at the syscall level (EPERM on internalConnectMultiple). Independently reproduced by running `npx stryker run stryker.baseline.config.mjs --concurrency 1 --dryRunOnly`. The captured: false state is preserved in baseline/mutation-score.json. Phase 10's stated rationale is that this capture MUST happen before Phase 11 deletes src/engine/pipeline/; deferring it forward defeats the phase's purpose.

- .ridgeline/builds/fascicle-migration/phase-10-mutation-score.json: Post-migration Stryker mutation score on src/engine/{flows,atoms,composites,adapters}/**/*.ts was not captured. Same root cause as criterion 2 — Stryker cannot run under the active sandbox. phase-10-mutation-score.json contains placeholders only (captured: false, score: null).

- scripts/phase-10-mutation-gate.mjs: The mutation gate script exits 0 with status DEFERRED whenever either input is captured: false. Per AC4 the assertion must compare two numbers and fail the phase exit if the new score is lower — but with no numbers present, no comparison is possible and the gate trivially passes. As written, the phase could ship with the scores never captured at all, which contradicts the spec's explicit ordering rationale.

Retrying (1/2) — Phase 10 fails: Stryker mutation testing — the foundational tool this entire phase is built around — could not be executed under the active sandbox. Both required workarounds (RIDGELINE_SANDBOX=0 and vitest pool: 'forks') were attempted and verified non-functional; AC2 and AC3 are not numerically satisfied (both score files have captured: false, score: null). AC4's gate script accepts this DEFERRED state and exits 0, so no numeric assertion is ever made. AC1, AC5, AC6, AC7, AC8, AC9 pass. The spec is explicit that the absolute pre-migration score MUST be captured at this phase before Phase 11 deletes src/engine/pipeline/ — that capture has not happened.
